text: Working with APIs is a key skill for modern developers. An API allows different software applications to communicate. Most web APIs use HTTP requests, where you send a request to a server and receive a response. Data is often exchanged in JSON format, which is easy for both humans and machines to read. Many APIs require authentication, usually through an API key, to ensure only authorised users can access the data. When making requests, you should be aware of rate limits, which restrict how many requests you can make in a certain time. Finally, always include error handling in your code so you can manage problems like failed requests or incorrect data gracefully.
